 {"id":"xf-30","title":"Add did-you-mean suggestions for typos","description":"## Task

When users mistype command names or type values, suggest the correct option using fuzzy matching.

## Current State

'Unknown command: serach' or 'Unknown type: twet'
No suggestion for what they probably meant.

## Target State

Unknown command: 'serach'

  Did you mean 'search'?
  
  Type 'help' for available commands.

## Implementation

1. Implement Levenshtein distance function or use strsim crate
2. When parsing fails, find closest match within edit distance of 2
3. Suggest the match if confidence is high enough

fn suggest_similar(input: &str, options: &[&str]) -> Option<&str> {
    options.iter()
        .filter_map(|opt| {
            let dist = strsim::levenshtein(input, opt);
            if dist <= 2 { Some((opt, dist)) } else { None }
        })
        .min_by_key(|(_, dist)| *dist)
        .map(|(opt, _)| *opt)
}

## Files to Modify

- Cargo.toml: Add strsim = "0.10" if not present
- src/repl.rs: parse_command() error handling
- src/cli.rs or main.rs: Type parsing errors

## Acceptance Criteria

- Typos within 2 edits suggest correct spelling
- Only suggests if there's a clear match
- Works for commands, types, and targets","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:52:06.676356-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T10:43:55.792301-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T10:43:55.792301-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented Levenshtein distance in error.rs and integrated did-you-mean suggestions for --fields and config keys. Used native implementation (no strsim dependency).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-30","depends_on_id":"xf-15","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"xf-31","title":"Fix CSV export missing carriage return handling","description":"## Bug

The CSV export in repl.rs replaces \n with space but leaves \r intact, which can cause issues in some spreadsheet software.

## Current Code (repl.rs:887)

let text_escaped = r.text.replace('"', """").replace('\n', " ");

## Fix

let text_escaped = r.text.replace('"', """").replace('\n', " ").replace('\r', "");

Or better:
let text_escaped = r.text.replace('"', """").replace(['\n', '\r'], " ");

## Files to Modify

- src/repl.rs: run_export() function
- src/main.rs: CSV output sections (if any)

## Testing

- Export data with Windows-style line endings (\r\n)
- Verify CSV opens correctly in Excel and other spreadsheet software

## Acceptance Criteria

- No \r characters in CSV output
- CSV is valid RFC 4180 format","notes":"CSV escaping now uses shared csv_escape_text across search output, export (serde-driven CSV), and REPL export; CR/LF sanitized and quotes escaped consistently. Unit tests cover CR/LF/quote handling; cli_e2e tests exercised with --nocapture.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-11T09:52:19.047685-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-11T11:33:46.822693307-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T11:33:46.822693307-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"xf-31","depends_on_id":"xf-19","type":"blocks","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z"}]}

-{"id":"xf-33","title":"Epic: Search & Indexing Performance Optimization","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:31:47.343553093-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T00:31:47.343553093-05:00"}
+{"id":"xf-33","title":"Epic: Search & Indexing Performance Optimization","description":"## Epic: Search & Indexing Performance Optimization

### Executive Summary
This epic tracks a comprehensive performance overhaul of xf's search and indexing subsystems. The goal is to achieve sub-50ms hybrid search latency and <2 minute indexing for 50K+ document archives while maintaining perfect output isomorphism (identical results before and after optimization).

### Background
Performance profiling revealed five critical bottlenecks in the current implementation:

1. **Embedding Reload per Search (CRITICAL)** - Every search call triggers a full table scan of embeddings (~73MB for 100K docs), rebuilding the VectorIndex from scratch. This adds 500-2000ms latency per search.

2. **N+1 Document Lookups (CRITICAL)** - After vector similarity search returns doc IDs, we fetch each document individually in a loop. For 100 results, this is 100 separate Tantivy queries instead of 1 batch query.

3. **Sequential Embedding Generation (CRITICAL)** - During indexing, embeddings are generated one-at-a-time despite being CPU-bound and embarrassingly parallel.

4. **Stats N+1 Queries (MEDIUM)** - The stats command runs 10+ separate COUNT(*) queries instead of consolidating into a single query.

5. **RRF Fusion Clones (MEDIUM)** - The hybrid search RRF fusion algorithm clones SearchResult objects unnecessarily, creating ~400 heap allocations per search.

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Hybrid search latency: <50ms (currently 500-2000ms)
- [ ] Lexical-only search latency: <20ms
- [ ] Semantic-only search latency: <30ms  
- [ ] Index 50K docs with embeddings: <2 minutes
- [ ] Stats command latency: <100ms
- [ ] Memory usage: <200MB peak for 100K doc index
- [ ] Output isomorphism: All outputs identical to pre-optimization baseline

### Risk Mitigation
- Comprehensive baseline capture before any changes
- Unit tests for each optimization with isomorphism verification
- E2E test script for full regression testing
- Detailed logging for debugging any issues

### Child Features
- xf-35: Embedding caching (P1)
- xf-36: Batch document lookup (P1)
- xf-37: Parallel embedding generation (P1)
- xf-38: Stats query consolidation (P2)
- xf-39: RRF clone elimination (P2)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:31:47.343553093-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T01:07:55.89260838-05:00"}
